panels.description:
Build preparation duration, broken down by sub-steps.

These steps are required to bootstrap the build and are reflected in the Codefresh UI as “Initializing Process.”
panels.title: Preparation duration

panels.description:
Build finalization duration, broken down by sub-steps.

These steps are required to gracefully terminate build execution and are not reflected in the Codefresh UI.
panels.title: Finalization duration

panels.description: Memory resources allocated to the build (`dind` pod).
panels.title: Build memory

panels.description:
Total build (`dind` pod) memory utilization as a percent of requests/limits.

If utilization is close to 100%, it indicates that the build is using all of its allocated memory, which may lead to performance issues or OOM (Out of Memory) kills.

If utilization is close to 0%, it indicates that the build is not using much memory, which may suggest over-provisioning.
panels.title: Build memory utilization

panels.description:
Total build (`dind` pod) CPU utilization as a percent of requests/limits.

If utilization is close to 100%, it indicates that the build is using all of its allocated CPU, which may lead to performance issues.

If utilization is close to 0%, it indicates that the build is not using much CPU, which may suggest over-provisioning.
panels.title: Build CPU utilization

panels.description:
Share of throttled CPU periods for the build (`dind` pod) as a percent of the total CPU periods.

If the value is above 0, it indicates that the build is being throttled due to CPU limits set on the `dind` pod. This can lead to slower build times and should be monitored to ensure that the pod has sufficient CPU resources.
panels.title: Build CPU throttling

panels.description: CPU resources allocated to the build (`dind` pod).
panels.title: Build CPU

panels.description:
Total build (attached volume) storage utilization.

If utilization is at 80%, in-DinD cleaner may be triggered (configurable, [docs](https://artifacthub.io/packages/helm/codefresh-runner/cf-runtime#volume-cleaners)).

If it's close to 100%, the build may fail because of Out Of Storage.

If utilization is close to 0%, it indicates that the build is not using much storage, which may suggest over-provisioning.
panels.title: Storage utilization

panels.description:
Total build (attached volume) index nodes utilization.

If utilization is at 80%, in-DinD cleaner may be triggered (configurable, [docs](https://artifacthub.io/packages/helm/codefresh-runner/cf-runtime#volume-cleaners)).

If it's close to 100%, the build may fail because of Out Of Storage.
panels.title: Inodes utilization

panels.description:
Step names are displayed as defined in the pipeline YAML.

Each step is clickable, allowing you to navigate directly to the corresponding step in Codefresh.

Duration of the steps is shown with $min_interval precision, which is controlled by “Min interval” variable.
panels.title: Steps timeline

panels.description:
Step durations, broken down by phases:

* `preparing`: Internal preparation time before the actual step begins.
* `running`: Time taken to execute the step.
* `finalizing`: Time spent on internal finalization after the step is executed.

Step names are displayed as defined in the pipeline YAML.

Each step is clickable, allowing you to navigate directly to the corresponding step in Codefresh.

value.title: Go to the step in Codefresh

panels.title: Steps duration

panels.description:
Memory consumption broken down by step, stacked.

Step names are displayed as defined in the pipeline YAML.

Each step is clickable, allowing you to navigate directly to the corresponding step in Codefresh.

“Build pod memory” is the total memory used by the `dind` pod, which includes the memory used by the Docker daemon itself and any step containers running inside it.
panels.title: Steps memory

panels.description:
CPU consumption broken down by step, stacked.

Step names are displayed as defined in the pipeline YAML.

Each step is clickable, allowing you to navigate directly to the corresponding step in Codefresh.

“Build pod CPU” is the total CPU used by the `dind` pod, which includes the CPU used by the Docker daemon itself and any step containers running inside it.
panels.title: Steps CPU

panels.description: Memory resources allocated to the `engine` pod.
panels.title: Engine memory

panels.description:
Total `engine` pod memory utilization as a percent of requests/limits.

The `engine` is responsible for steps orchestration and execution.

If utilization is close to 100%, it indicates that the `engine` pod is using all of its allocated memory, which may lead to performance issues or OOM (Out of Memory) kills.

If utilization is close to 0%, it indicates that the `engine` pod is not using much memory, which may suggest over-provisioning.
panels.title: Engine memory utilization

panels.description:
Total `engine` pod CPU utilization as a percent of requests/limits.

The `engine` is responsible for steps orchestration and execution.

If utilization is close to 100%, it indicates that the `engine` pod is using all of its allocated CPU, which may lead to performance issues.

If utilization is close to 0%, it indicates that the `engine` pod is not using much CPU, which may suggest over-provisioning.
panels.title: Engine CPU utilization

panels.description:
Share of throttled CPU periods for the `engine` pod as a percent of the total CPU periods.

If the value is above 0, it indicates that the `engine` pod is being throttled due to CPU limits set on the pod. This can lead to slower build times and should be monitored to ensure that the pod has sufficient CPU resources.
panels.title: Engine CPU throttling

panels.description: CPU resources allocated to the `engine` pod.
panels.title: Build CPU

list.description: A lower limit for the interval. Recommended to be set to write frequency, for example `1m` if your data is written every minute.
list.label: Min interval
list.name: min_interval

list.description: Codefresh root URL. Will be used to provide external links in dashboard. No trailing slash allowed.
list.label: Codefresh URL
list.name: codefresh_url

list.description: Open build in Codefresh as Platform Admin
list.label: Admin view
list.name: no_account

list.description: Account to which the build belongs to
list.label: Account
list.name: cf_account_name

list.description: Cluster in which the build runs
list.label: Cluster
list.name: cluster

list.description: Namespace in which the build runs
list.label: Namespace
list.name: k8s_namespace_name

list.description: Pipeline in which the build runs. In the format `<project-name>/<pipeline-name>`
list.label: Pipeline
list.name: cf_classic_pipeline_name
